Arrays / Hashing.cue: Need fast lookup, deduplication, or frequency counting over a flat list
common_traps: Using a list for membership checks (O(n)) instead of a set (O(1))
Forgetting to handle empty input
Off-by-one on index ranges

Two Pointers.cue: Sorted array or palindrome check; need to compare or sum elements from both ends
common_traps: Not sorting the array first when required
Moving both pointers at the same time
Infinite loop when neither pointer moves

Sliding Window.cue: Longest or shortest contiguous subarray/substring satisfying a constraint
common_traps: Moving left pointer one step at a time instead of jumping past duplicate
Forgetting to shrink the window when the constraint is violated
Not updating the result after shrinking

Stack.cue: Need to match pairs (brackets, tags), track running minimum/maximum, or find the next greater/smaller element
common_traps: Not checking if the stack is empty before popping
Using a regular stack when a monotonic stack is needed
Forgetting to flush the stack after the main loop

Binary Search.cue: Sorted array with a target; or minimize/maximize a value over a monotonic condition
common_traps: Off-by-one: using mid+1 vs mid, left<right vs left<=right
Overflow on mid = (left+right)/2 (use left + (right-left)//2)
Not defining what left/right represent before coding

Linked List.cue: In-place reversal, cycle detection, or nth-from-end in a singly linked list
common_traps: Losing a pointer before reassigning (save next before overwriting)
Not handling the head = None edge case
Off-by-one in two-pointer (slow/fast) for middle finding

Intervals.cue: Given a list of intervals, merge, insert, or count overlaps
common_traps: Forgetting to sort by start time first
Off-by-one on overlap condition: use c <= b not c < b for overlap
Not initializing result with the first interval

Trees.cue: Traverse, search, or modify a binary tree; find depth, diameter, or level order
common_traps: Not handling null children before recursing
Confusing inorder/preorder/postorder when the order matters
Diameter error: max path is not always through root

Graphs.cue: Connected components, shortest path, cycle detection, or topological ordering in a grid or adjacency list
common_traps: Forgetting to mark nodes visited before pushing to queue (BFS double-visit)
Not building the adjacency list from edge list input
Assuming graph is connected when it may have isolated nodes

Heap / Priority Queue.cue: Need the k-th largest/smallest, or always need the current minimum/maximum efficiently
common_traps: Using max-heap when Python's heapq is a min-heap (negate values for max)
Pushing duplicates into heap when uniqueness is needed
O(n log n) heap build vs O(n) heapify — use heapify for initial construction

Backtracking.cue: Generate all permutations, subsets, or valid combinations that satisfy a constraint
common_traps: Forgetting to undo state (backtrack) after the recursive call
Not pruning early when the constraint is already violated
Appending a reference to the current list instead of a copy

Dynamic Programming.cue: Optimal substructure: count ways, maximize/minimize a value, or find longest subsequence
common_traps: Not defining the DP state clearly before writing the recurrence
Off-by-one on dp array size (often need dp[n+1] for length n)
Forgetting the base case initializations before filling the table
